NCT02207231.name: VOYAGE 1
NCT02207231.group: Moderate-severe plaque psoriasis, guselkumab 100 mg SC vs placebo (week 16)
NCT02207231.snippet: Source: Blauvelt A et al. J Am Acad Dermatol 2017;76:405-417 (VOYAGE 1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 837 moderate-severe plaque psoriasis adults (BSA >=10%, PASI >=12, IGA >=3) were randomized double-blind to guselkumab 100 mg SC week 0, 4, then Q8W (n=329), adalimumab 80/40 mg (n=334), or placebo (n=174).

evidence.label: Primary — PASI 90 at Week 16
evidence.text: Proportion achieving PASI 90 at week 16: guselkumab 73.3% (241/329) vs placebo 2.9% (5/174); odds ratio 25.80 (95% CI 10.53 to 63.20; P<0.001).

evidence.label: IGA Clear / Almost Clear
evidence.text: IGA 0/1 (clear or almost clear) at week 16: guselkumab 85.1% (280/329) vs placebo 6.9% (12/174); OR 27.0 (95% CI 14.2-51.3; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW — central stratified randomization. D2 LOW — double-blind placebo with matched schedule. D3 LOW — >95% completion. D4 LOW — central PASI/IGA scoring by trained blinded assessors. D5 LOW — SAP pre-locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 99 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Have a diagnosis of plaque-type psoriasis (with or without psoriatic arthritis) at least 6 months before the first administration of study agent~* Have a Psoriasis Area and Severity Index (PASI) greater than or equal to (>=) 12 at Screening and at Baseline~* Have an Investigator's Global Assessment (IGA) score >=3 at Screening and at Baseline~* Have an involved body surface area (BSA) >=10 percent (%) at Screening and at Baseline~* Must be a candidate for either systemi...

NCT02684370.name: UltIMMa-1
NCT02684370.group: Moderate-severe plaque psoriasis, risankizumab 150 mg SC vs placebo (week 16)
NCT02684370.snippet: Source: Gordon KB et al. Lancet 2018;392:650-661 (UltIMMa-1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 506 adults with moderate-to-severe chronic plaque psoriasis randomized 3:1:1 double-blind to risankizumab 150 mg SC weeks 0, 4, then Q12W (n=304), ustekinumab 45/90 mg (n=100), or placebo (n=102).

evidence.label: Primary — PASI 90 at Week 16
evidence.text: Co-primary PASI 90 at week 16: risankizumab 75.3% (229/304) vs placebo 4.9% (5/102); odds ratio 59.21 (95% CI 23.38 to 149.88; P<0.0001).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW — central randomization stratified by weight and prior biologic. D2 LOW — double-blind with matching placebo schedule. D3 LOW — >98% follow-up. D4 LOW — central blinded dermatology reader panel. D5 LOW — SAP lock.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~* Male or female patients with age ≥18 years at screening.~* Have a diagnosis of chronic plaque psoriasis (with or without psoriatic arthritis) for at least 6 months before the first administration of study drug.~* Have stable moderate to severe chronic plaque psoriasis with or without psoriatic arthritis at both Screening and Baseline (Randomisation):~  1. Have an involved body surface area (BSA) ≥10% and~ 2. Have a Psoriasis Area and Severity Index (PASI) score ≥12 and~ 3....

NCT01722331.name: reSURFACE 1
NCT01722331.group: Moderate-severe plaque psoriasis, tildrakizumab 200 mg SC vs placebo (week 12)
NCT01722331.snippet: Source: Reich K et al. Lancet 2017;390:276-288 (reSURFACE 1 tildrakizumab).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 772 moderate-severe plaque psoriasis adults randomized 2:2:1 double-blind to tildrakizumab 100 mg SC (n=309), tildrakizumab 200 mg SC (n=308), or placebo (n=154) at weeks 0 and 4, then Q12W maintenance.

evidence.label: Primary — PASI 75 and PASI 90 at Week 12
evidence.text: Co-primary PASI 75 at week 12: tildrakizumab 200 mg 62% (192/308) vs placebo 6% (9/154). Secondary PASI 90: 35% (107/308) vs <1% (1/154). For the pre-specified PASI 90 pool endpoint: OR 48.38 (95% CI 6.66 to 351.4; P<0.001).

evidence.label: PGA 0/1 Clear / Minimal
evidence.text: PGA 0 or 1 at week 12: tildrakizumab 200 mg 61% (188/308) vs placebo 7% (11/154); OR 13.73 (95% CI 7.13-26.43; P<0.0001).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW — central stratified randomization. D2 LOW — double-blind placebo-matching schedule. D3 LOW — 94% completion. D4 LOW — central blinded PASI/PGA scoring. D5 LOW — SAP locked, reporting for all pre-specified outcomes.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Clinical diagnosis of moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis for at least 6 months prior to study enrollment~* A candidate for phototherapy or systemic therapy~* For the extension study: must have completed Part 3 of the base study~* For the extension study: must have achieved at least a PASI-50 response by the end of Part 3 of the base study~* For the extension study: must have received active tildrakizumab (MK-3222) treatment within 12 weeks prior to the end of Part 3 of th...

title: RapidMeta Dermatology | IL-23 Inhibitors for Plaque Psoriasis v1.0
